title: Bridge Paper: Negative impact of daily screen use on inhibitory control network in preadolescence: A two-year follow-up study.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Habit — automaticity, habitual behavior, action monitoring, loss of conscious control.
**Phase context:** habit, automaticity, habitual behavior, action monitoring, chunked behavior

Paper: Negative impact of daily screen use on inhibitory control network in preadolescence: A two-year follow-up study.
DOI: 10.1016/j.dcn.2023.101218
Abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic has made an unprecedented shift in children's daily lives. Children are increasingly spending time with screens to learn and connect with others. As the online environment rapidly substitutes in-person experience, understanding children's neuropsychological trajectories associated with screen experiences is important.
